prompt: Ukiyo-e woodblock print in the style of Katsushika Hokusai, Edo period Japan. A lone traveller descending a steep mountain path in a winding, spiralling route toward a valley far below — the path curves and zigzags downward following the natural contours of the terrain, always moving toward the lowest point. The mountain is rendered in flat unmodulated areas of indigo, grey and ochre. Bold black outlines define every form. No shading or photographic gradients — only flat color fills separated by sharp ink lines. Mount Fuji visible in the background. Visible woodgrain texture throughout. No text, no labels, no modern elements.
tradition_approach: Targeted Hokusai's flat unmodulated color fills, bold defining ink outlines, woodgrain texture, diagonal compositional movement, and muted indigo-ochre palette — all period-authentic conventions absent from generic 'Japanese-style' images.
